title: Liquidity Provider Retreat Under Toxicity
focus_questions: Did passive size disappear because it was fake, or because defending there became too toxic?
Where did the provider actually reprice or retreat to?

summary.thesis: Passive liquidity can retreat or reprice under toxic one-way flow, so disappearance at the old price is not automatically fake depth and not automatically a good defense level anymore.
summary.operator_translation: Do not keep leaning on an old defended price after the provider has clearly changed state under pressure.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when a previously real defending side begins retreating, repricing, or standing deeper as adverse-selection pressure rises.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: previously real passive defense starts changing behavior under toxic one-way flow. Required evidence: prior defense was real, pressure rises, and the provider retreats or reprices rather than standing at the old price. Do not trade: by assuming the old DOM wall still matters or by calling every retreat spoofing. Real trigger: recognition of the new defended area or failure of the old area after retreat. Failure point: the provider re-establishes real defense at the old price.

when_relevant: A previously real defender was visible at an important level.
One-way pressure becomes more toxic or more one-sided.
The passive side stops standing at the old price and begins retreating or repricing.
required_evidence: there was prior evidence of real defense at the old price
aggressive one-way pressure or toxicity increased
the passive side retreated, repriced, or defended deeper instead of staying at the old level
confirmation_signals: a previously real defender stops standing at the old price
one-way pressure rises while passive quotes retreat or reprice
the market begins referencing a new defended area instead of the old one
invalidation_signals: the old price is re-defended with real outcome
the supposed retreat was only momentary and no new reference price formed
pressure does not remain toxic enough to justify a state change
no_trade_conditions: Do not assume static DOM size will stay when toxicity rises.
Do not label every retreat as spoofing without branch context.
Do not keep leaning on the old defended price after the provider clearly repriced.

entry_archetypes.name: old defense failure after retreat
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the old defended price clearly fails because the provider no longer stands there.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the retreat is temporary and the old level quickly re-establishes.

entry_archetypes.name: new reprice defense follow
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the provider's deeper or repriced level begins creating real outcome.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the new reference price is still cosmetic and unproven.

management_notes: This card is strongest when prior defense was real and later changes state.
Retreat and reprice can still leave a new level worth following.
Hand off to real support or resistance logic once the new reference price is proven.
review_questions: Was the provider baiting or rationally retreating under pressure?
Did the operator keep trusting the old price after the defense already moved?
Where did the new defended reference actually appear?
